entries.title: Carboplatin De-escalation
entries.subtitle: neoCARHP · Breast cancer · Early HER2-positive
entries.summary: Similar pathologic complete response while omitting carboplatin. Approximately 10 fewer grade 3-4 neutropenia events and 9 fewer severe leukopenia events per 100 patients.
entries.headline: 64.1% vs 65.9% pCR · Noninferior response with lower severe hematologic toxicity
entries.searchText: carboplatin de-escalation neocarhp breast cancer early her2-positive roche + generic manufacturers phase iii high, pending survival 64.1% vs 65.9% pcr noninferior response with lower severe hematologic toxicity similar pathologic complete response while omitting carboplatin. approximately 10 fewer grade 3-4 neutropenia events and 9 fewer severe leukopenia events per 100 patients. lower neutropenia, leukopenia, thrombocytopenia, anemia, nausea and treatment interruption. long-term event-free and overall survival remain immature.
sections: 64.1% vs 65.9% pCR. Noninferior response with lower severe hematologic toxicity
Similar pathologic complete response while omitting carboplatin. Approximately 10 fewer grade 3-4 neutropenia events and 9 fewer severe leukopenia events per 100 patients.
Lower neutropenia, leukopenia, thrombocytopenia, anemia, nausea and treatment interruption. Long-term event-free and overall survival remain immature.

Cost-saving strategy. Components available: carboplatin ₹1,900-₹2,800; trastuzumab biosimilars ₹17,700-₹57,500; Perjeta ₹2.52-₹2.62 lakh per vial. Treatment approach still requires guideline and clinician adoption.
No new product launch needed. Available
pCR is a surrogate. Survival follow-up and high-risk subgroup selection are still required.

entries.title: Tucatinib + HER2 Maintenance
entries.subtitle: Tucatinib · Breast cancer · Metastatic HER2-positive
entries.summary: A direct randomized improvement over trastuzumab/pertuzumab maintenance, with a 35.9% relative reduction in progression or death. Benefit appeared across hormone-receptor and brain-metastasis subgroups.
entries.headline: +8.6 months median PFS · 24.9 vs 16.3 months; HR 0.641
entries.searchText: tucatinib + her2 maintenance tucatinib breast cancer metastatic her2-positive pfizer / seagen; indian generics phase iii high +8.6 months median pfs 24.9 vs 16.3 months; hr 0.641 a direct randomized improvement over trastuzumab/pertuzumab maintenance, with a 35.9% relative reduction in progression or death. benefit appeared across hormone-receptor and brain-metastasis subgroups. diarrhea in 72.7%, grade 3 or higher in 6.1%. grade 3 or higher alt and ast elevations were 13.5% and 7.1%; 13.5% discontinued for treatment-emergent toxicity. tukysa perjeta
sections: +8.6 months median PFS. 24.9 vs 16.3 months; HR 0.641

A direct randomized improvement over trastuzumab/pertuzumab maintenance, with a 35.9% relative reduction in progression or death. Benefit appeared across hormone-receptor and brain-metastasis subgroups.
Diarrhea in 72.7%, grade 3 or higher in 6.1%. Grade 3 or higher ALT and AST elevations were 13.5% and 7.1%; 13.5% discontinued for treatment-emergent toxicity.

High added continuous cost. ₹6,000 retail or ₹7,678-₹8,190 MRP per 10 × 150 mg tablets; about ₹72,000-₹98,000 per 30 days at 300 mg twice daily. Indian approval is for previously treated disease with trastuzumab and capecitabine, not HER2CLIMB-05 first-line maintenance.
Launched after 8 April 2025 approval. Approved and marketed
Overall survival is immature; long-term value depends on CNS-event avoidance, treatment duration and net pricing.

entries.title: Neoadjuvant Durvalumab
entries.subtitle: Durvalumab · Breast cancer · Early triple-negative
entries.summary: Improved invasive disease-free, distant disease-free and overall survival despite no decisive overall pCR improvement. Suggests durable immune priming may be possible without routine adjuvant continuation.
entries.headline: OS HR 0.33 · Long-term follow-up at median 86.4 months
entries.searchText: neoadjuvant durvalumab durvalumab breast cancer early triple-negative astrazeneca phase ii moderate-high os hr 0.33 long-term follow-up at median 86.4 months improved invasive disease-free, distant disease-free and overall survival despite no decisive overall pcr improvement. suggests durable immune priming may be possible without routine adjuvant continuation. checkpoint blockade can cause permanent endocrinopathies and serious immune pneumonitis, hepatitis, colitis and other organ toxicity. imfinzi
sections: OS HR 0.33. Long-term follow-up at median 86.4 months

Improved invasive disease-free, distant disease-free and overall survival despite no decisive overall pCR improvement. Suggests durable immune priming may be possible without routine adjuvant continuation.
Checkpoint blockade can cause permanent endocrinopathies and serious immune pneumonitis, hepatitis, colitis and other organ toxicity.

High; shorter duration could save cost. Imfinzi 500 mg revised MRP ₹1.713 lakh; pharmacy listings approximately ₹1.47-₹1.71 lakh. The GeparNuevo early-TNBC use is not an established Indian labeled indication.
Small phase II trial and no direct comparison with current perioperative pembrolizumab standard.

entries.title: Personalized mRNA Neoantigen Therapy
entries.subtitle: Intismeran · Melanoma · Resected high-risk
entries.summary: A large and durable recurrence-free survival signal from patient-specific tumor neoantigens. Five-year distant metastasis-free survival also favored combination treatment.
entries.headline: +19.7 points 5-year RFS · 68.8% vs 49.1%; RFS HR 0.51
entries.searchText: personalized mrna neoantigen therapy intismeran melanoma resected high-risk moderna + merck / msd phase iib potentially transformative +19.7 points 5-year rfs 68.8% vs 49.1%; rfs hr 0.51 a large and durable recurrence-free survival signal from patient-specific tumor neoantigens. five-year distant metastasis-free survival also favored combination treatment. no new signal reported; injection-related symptoms and pembrolizumab immune toxicity remain relevant. tissue adequacy and manufacturing delay add nonmedical burden. keytruda mrna-4157 v940
sections: A large and durable recurrence-free survival signal from patient-specific tumor neoantigens. Five-year distant metastasis-free survival also favored combination treatment.
No new signal reported; injection-related symptoms and pembrolizumab immune toxicity remain relevant. Tissue adequacy and manufacturing delay add nonmedical burden.
Unknown; likely very high initially. No commercial price. Requires positive phase III results, global filing, individualized manufacturing validation and CDSCO review.
No announced date; late-2020s at earliest is an inference. Not launched
Phase IIb sample size, small control group and nondefinitive overall survival. Phase III confirmation is ongoing.

entries.title: First-line Pemigatinib
entries.subtitle: Pemigatinib · Cholangiocarcinoma · FGFR2-rearranged
entries.snapshot: Phase III · High in biomarker subset
entries.summary: Improved response and progression-free survival over chemotherapy in a molecularly selected population. The result supports early broad genomic testing.
entries.headline: ORR 47% vs 15% · PFS 8.3 vs 6.8 months
entries.searchText: first-line pemigatinib pemigatinib cholangiocarcinoma fgfr2-rearranged incyte phase iii high in biomarker subset orr 47% vs 15% pfs 8.3 vs 6.8 months improved response and progression-free survival over chemotherapy in a molecularly selected population. the result supports early broad genomic testing. fgfr inhibition requires monitoring for hyperphosphatemia, ocular toxicity, nail and skin changes, stomatitis and dose interruption. pemazyre
sections: Improved response and progression-free survival over chemotherapy in a molecularly selected population. The result supports early broad genomic testing.
FGFR inhibition requires monitoring for hyperphosphatemia, ocular toxicity, nail and skin changes, stomatitis and dose interruption.

Very high. No verified Indian MRP. US $20,286 per 14 tablets converts to about ₹19.4 lakh, or about ₹3.34 crore annually; not an Indian selling price. Named-patient import may be possible; first-line positioning requires indication-specific approval.
No public India date. No verified domestic launch
Applies to a small FGFR2-rearranged subset and depends on timely, high-quality molecular testing.

entries.title: Amivantamab in Refractory CRC
entries.subtitle: Amivantamab · Colorectal cancer · RAS/BRAF wild-type
entries.summary: Activity was notable after prior anti-EGFR therapy and in right-sided disease, where conventional EGFR antibodies can be less reliable.
entries.headline: ORR 19%–29% · Median PFS 3.7–5.7 months
entries.searchText: amivantamab in refractory crc amivantamab colorectal cancer ras/braf wild-type johnson & johnson / janssen phase ib/ii promising, not practice-changing orr 19%–29% median pfs 3.7–5.7 months activity was notable after prior anti-egfr therapy and in right-sided disease, where conventional egfr antibodies can be less reliable. grade 3 or higher rash 7%, acneiform dermatitis 4% and hypoalbuminemia 4%; infusion reactions and skin toxicity add operational burden. rybrevant
sections: Activity was notable after prior anti-EGFR therapy and in right-sided disease, where conventional EGFR antibodies can be less reliable.
Grade 3 or higher rash 7%, acneiform dermatitis 4% and hypoalbuminemia 4%; infusion reactions and skin toxicity add operational burden.
Very high. US $3,894 per 350 mg vial converts to about ₹3.72 lakh per vial; not an Indian market price. Rybrevant approvals in other tumor settings do not establish colorectal-cancer use.
No public India date for colorectal cancer. Trial / import context
Small nonrandomized cohorts, biomarker-intensive selection, no survival comparison and high acquisition cost.

entries.title: Integrin Beta-6 ADC
entries.subtitle: Sigvotatug · Lung cancer · Advanced NSCLC
entries.summary: The taxane-naive nonsquamous subgroup signal appears favorable to historical docetaxel expectations, with long response duration among responders.
entries.headline: ORR 29% in subgroup · PFS 6.4 months; response duration 12.8 months
entries.searchText: integrin beta-6 adc sigvotatug lung cancer advanced nsclc pfizer / seagen phase i promising orr 29% in subgroup pfs 6.4 months; response duration 12.8 months the taxane-naive nonsquamous subgroup signal appears favorable to historical docetaxel expectations, with long response duration among responders. peripheral sensory neuropathy is a key cumulative toxicity. at the selected schedule, 19% discontinued for treatment-emergent events.
sections: ORR 29% in subgroup. PFS 6.4 months; response duration 12.8 months
The taxane-naive nonsquamous subgroup signal appears favorable to historical docetaxel expectations, with long response duration among responders.
Peripheral sensory neuropathy is a key cumulative toxicity. At the selected schedule, 19% discontinued for treatment-emergent events.
Unknown; likely high ADC cost. No commercial price. Phase III efficacy, safety and subsequent global and CDSCO approvals are required.
No announced date; late-2020s at earliest is an inference. Not launched
First-in-human, heterogeneous dosing and populations, historical comparator and no validated routine selection biomarker.

entries.title: Neoadjuvant Sacituzumab Govitecan
entries.subtitle: Sacituzumab · Bladder cancer · Cisplatin-ineligible MIBC
entries.summary: Provides proof that TROP2-directed therapy is active in muscle-invasive bladder cancer and may support future subtype-guided ADC selection.
entries.headline: ypT0N0-x 29.5% · 24-month event-free survival 71.4%
entries.statusLabel: Imported product; study use not launched
badges: Imported product; study use not launched
entries.searchText: neoadjuvant sacituzumab govitecan sacituzumab bladder cancer cisplatin-ineligible mibc gilead sciences phase ii exploratory ypt0n0-x 29.5% 24-month event-free survival 71.4% provides proof that trop2-directed therapy is active in muscle-invasive bladder cancer and may support future subtype-guided adc selection. neutropenia, diarrhea and infection are central concerns. two early deaths led to dose reduction and prophylaxis; one death was treatment-related. trodelvy
sections: Provides proof that TROP2-directed therapy is active in muscle-invasive bladder cancer and may support future subtype-guided ADC selection.
Neutropenia, diarrhea and infection are central concerns. Two early deaths led to dose reduction and prophylaxis; one death was treatment-related.
High. Import listings approximately ₹1.85-₹1.90 lakh per 180–200 mg vial; supplier quotes, not official MRP. The SURE-01 regimen needs comparative confirmation and regulatory review.
No public India date for this indication. Imported product; study use not launched
Small single-arm evidence, protocol changes, incomplete cystectomy assessment and a stronger contemporary benchmark.

entries.title: Durvalumab + FLOT
entries.subtitle: MATTERHORN · Gastric / GEJ cancer
entries.summary: The ASCO signal completed the conference-to-label pathway within six months. FDA authorized perioperative durvalumab with FLOT followed by durvalumab for resectable gastric or GEJ adenocarcinoma.
entries.headline: At FDA review: median EFS not reached vs 32.8 months; HR 0.71 (95% CI 0.58–0.86). OS HR 0.78 (95% CI 0.63–0.96). pCR 19.2% vs 7.2%.
entries.searchText: durvalumab + flot matterhorn gastric / gej cancer astrazeneca fda approved phase iii efs benefit; recurrence or death risk reduced by 29%. fda approval · 25 nov 2025 at fda review: median efs not reached vs 32.8 months; hr 0.71 (95% ci 0.58–0.86). os hr 0.78 (95% ci 0.63–0.96). pcr 19.2% vs 7.2%. the asco signal completed the conference-to-label pathway within six months. fda authorized perioperative durvalumab with flot followed by durvalumab for resectable gastric or gej adenocarcinoma. immune-mediated adverse reactions and flot toxicity; fda notes the trial did not isolate the contribution of neoadjuvant versus adjuvant durvalumab. imfinzi
sections: At FDA review: median EFS not reached vs 32.8 months; HR 0.71 (95% CI 0.58–0.86). OS HR 0.78 (95% CI 0.63–0.96). pCR 19.2% vs 7.2%.
ASCO EFS signal matured to positive OS and a US label.

US: Approved 25 November 2025. Europe: Project Orbis partner reviews were ongoing in the FDA notice; current final EU status not verified in this review. India: Durvalumab is marketed in India, but CDSCO approval for this exact perioperative gastric/GEJ regimen was not verified.
Immune-mediated adverse reactions and FLOT toxicity; FDA notes the trial did not isolate the contribution of neoadjuvant versus adjuvant durvalumab.
Regional approvals, reimbursement and real-world uptake.
High: phase III publication plus FDA action.

entries.title: Trastuzumab deruxtecan + pertuzumab
entries.subtitle: DESTINY-Breast09 · Breast cancer
entries.summary: The regimen moved into the US first-line label. FDA reported median PFS of 40.7 versus 26.9 months, while overall survival was still immature at approval.
entries.headline: At FDA review: median PFS 40.7 vs 26.9 months; HR 0.56 (95% CI 0.44–0.71). ORR 87% vs 81%; OS immature.
entries.searchText: trastuzumab deruxtecan + pertuzumab destiny-breast09 breast cancer daiichi sankyo + astrazeneca fda approved first-line her2-positive metastatic breast cancer pfs benefit over thp. fda approval · 15 dec 2025 at fda review: median pfs 40.7 vs 26.9 months; hr 0.56 (95% ci 0.44–0.71). orr 87% vs 81%; os immature. the regimen moved into the us first-line label. fda reported median pfs of 40.7 versus 26.9 months, while overall survival was still immature at approval. t-dxd carries boxed warning for interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis; nausea, marrow toxicity and cardiac monitoring remain relevant. enhertu perjeta
sections: At FDA review: median PFS 40.7 vs 26.9 months; HR 0.56 (95% CI 0.44–0.71). ORR 87% vs 81%; OS immature.
Large PFS gain translated to a first-line US label; OS remains immature.

US: Approved with pertuzumab for first-line metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer on 15 December 2025. Europe: Current final status not verified in this review. India: Enhertu and pertuzumab access and exact first-line combination labeling require current CDSCO and market verification.
T-DXd carries boxed warning for interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis; nausea, marrow toxicity and cardiac monitoring remain relevant.
OS maturation, CNS outcomes, regional labels and reimbursement.
High: randomized phase III plus FDA action.

entries.title: Perioperative pembrolizumab
entries.subtitle: KEYNOTE-689 · Head and neck cancer
entries.summary: FDA approved the PD-L1 CPS ≥1 regimen immediately after ASCO: neoadjuvant pembrolizumab, adjuvant pembrolizumab with radiotherapy with or without cisplatin, then pembrolizumab alone.
entries.headline: FDA authorized the regimen for PD-L1 CPS ≥1; exact effect estimates should be read from the label/publication.
entries.searchText: perioperative pembrolizumab keynote-689 head and neck cancer merck / msd fda approved event-free survival improvement in resectable locally advanced hnscc. fda approval · 12 jun 2025 fda authorized the regimen for pd-l1 cps ≥1; exact effect estimates should be read from the label/publication. fda approved the pd-l1 cps ≥1 regimen immediately after asco: neoadjuvant pembrolizumab, adjuvant pembrolizumab with radiotherapy with or without cisplatin, then pembrolizumab alone. adds perioperative immune toxicity to surgery and radiotherapy; hypothyroidism, diarrhea and immune-mediated organ toxicity require monitoring. keytruda
sections: 714; resectable stage III–IVA locally advanced HNSCC
Surgery followed by standard adjuvant radiotherapy ± cisplatin
FDA authorized the regimen for PD-L1 CPS ≥1; exact effect estimates should be read from the label/publication.
Conference evidence translated almost immediately to the first US perioperative HNSCC approval.

US: Approved 12 June 2025 for PD-L1 CPS ≥1. Europe: Current final status not verified in this review. India: Pembrolizumab is marketed, but this exact perioperative HNSCC indication was not verified with CDSCO.
Adds perioperative immune toxicity to surgery and radiotherapy; hypothyroidism, diarrhea and immune-mediated organ toxicity require monitoring.
Long-term efficacy, regional approvals and implementation outside trial centers.
High: phase III plus FDA action.

entries.title: Rusfertide
entries.subtitle: VERIFY · Polycythemia vera
entries.summary: The molecule remains investigational but has crossed into formal regulatory review. FDA's target review period is Q3 2026; approval is not yet established.
entries.headline: More than doubled clinical response in the ASCO report; NDA includes 32- and 52-week VERIFY data plus long-term phase II evidence.
entries.searchText: rusfertide verify polycythemia vera takeda + protagonist therapeutics priority review weekly hepcidin mimetic more than doubled clinical response and reduced phlebotomy burden. fda nda priority review · 27 feb 2026 more than doubled clinical response in the asco report; nda includes 32- and 52-week verify data plus long-term phase ii evidence. the molecule remains investigational but has crossed into formal regulatory review. fda's target review period is q3 2026; approval is not yet established. weekly self-injection; generally tolerated in trials. full approved-label safety is unavailable because the product remains investigational. ptg-300
sections: 293; phlebotomy-dependent polycythemia vera despite standard care
More than doubled clinical response in the ASCO report; NDA includes 32- and 52-week VERIFY data plus long-term phase II evidence.
Moved from positive phase III to formal NDA review.
US: NDA accepted with priority review on 27 February 2026; target action in Q3 2026. Europe: No verified authorization. India: No verified launch, price or CDSCO filing.
Weekly self-injection; generally tolerated in trials. Full approved-label safety is unavailable because the product remains investigational.
High for review status; approval outcome unknown.

entries.title: Camizestrant + CDK4/6 inhibitor
entries.subtitle: SERENA-6 · Breast cancer
entries.snapshot: EU CHMP positive · 21 May 2026; US review unresolved
entries.summary: Europe's CHMP recommended approval, while FDA convened ODAC over uncertainty about whether switching before radiographic progression provides clinically meaningful benefit. No final US approval was verified by 9 June 2026.
entries.headline: Median PFS 16.0 vs 9.2 months; HR 0.44. The unresolved question is whether earlier switching improves outcomes compared with switching at clinical progression.
entries.searchText: camizestrant + cdk4/6 inhibitor serena-6 breast cancer astrazeneca regulatory divergence ctdna-guided switch at emergent esr1 mutation improved median pfs from 9.2 to 16.0 months. eu chmp positive · 21 may 2026; us review unresolved median pfs 16.0 vs 9.2 months; hr 0.44. the unresolved question is whether earlier switching improves outcomes compared with switching at clinical progression. europe's chmp recommended approval, while fda convened odac over uncertainty about whether switching before radiographic progression provides clinically meaningful benefit. no final us approval was verified by 9 june 2026. oral serd safety must be considered alongside long-term cdk4/6 inhibition; final label-specific safety is pending. azd9833
sections: Global randomized double-blind phase III; ctDNA-triggered switch
HR+/HER2− advanced breast cancer with emergent ESR1 mutation before radiographic progression
Continue aromatase inhibitor + same CDK4/6 inhibitor
Median PFS 16.0 vs 9.2 months; HR 0.44. The unresolved question is whether earlier switching improves outcomes compared with switching at clinical progression.
EU CHMP positive · 21 May 2026; US review unresolved
Strong PFS signal produced regulatory divergence rather than a uniform global conclusion.

US: NDA under review; FDA ODAC discussed uncertainty on 30 April 2026. No final approval verified by 9 June 2026. Europe: CHMP positive opinion on 21 May 2026; European Commission decision pending at review date. India: No verified approval or launch.
Oral SERD safety must be considered alongside long-term CDK4/6 inhibition; final label-specific safety is pending.
US FDA decision and European Commission authorization.
High for trial and committee status; final decisions pending.

entries.title: Atezolizumab + mFOLFOX6
entries.subtitle: ATOMIC · Colon cancer
entries.summary: The regimen entered NCCN guidance rapidly, but no FDA approval for this adjuvant dMMR colon indication was identified as of 9 June 2026. Guideline use and regulatory labeling are therefore not equivalent.
entries.headline: Three-year DFS 86.4% vs 76.6%; HR 0.50; p<0.0001.
entries.searchText: atezolizumab + mfolfox6 atomic colon cancer genentech / roche + nci alliance guideline signal three-year dfs 86.4% versus 76.6% in stage iii dmmr colon cancer; hr 0.50. nccn incorporation reported · jun 2025 three-year dfs 86.4% vs 76.6%; hr 0.50; p<0.0001. the regimen entered nccn guidance rapidly, but no fda approval for this adjuvant dmmr colon indication was identified as of 9 june 2026. guideline use and regulatory labeling are therefore not equivalent. adds checkpoint-inhibitor immune toxicity to six months of oxaliplatin-based chemotherapy. tecentriq
sections: Rapid guideline movement, but no verified regimen-specific regulatory approval.

US: Reported incorporation into NCCN Colon Cancer v4.2025; no FDA label for this indication verified by 9 June 2026. Europe: No verified regulatory authorization for this indication. India: Atezolizumab availability does not establish adjuvant dMMR colon approval; no exact CDSCO indication verified.
Adds checkpoint-inhibitor immune toxicity to six months of oxaliplatin-based chemotherapy.
Publication, OS maturation and regulatory filings.
High for ASCO data; guideline statement reported by ASCO Post.

entries.title: Postoperative nivolumab + chemoradiotherapy
entries.subtitle: NIVOPOSTOP · Head and neck cancer
entries.summary: The DFS signal persists in follow-up analyses, including across lymph-node dissection extent. No FDA approval for the NIVOPOSTOP regimen was verified; KEYNOTE-689 pembrolizumab has separately gained a US perioperative label.
entries.headline: DFS 63.1% vs 52.5% at just over 2.5 years; subsequent ASCO 2026 post-hoc analysis found benefit was not altered by lymph-node dissection extent.
entries.searchText: postoperative nivolumab + chemoradiotherapy nivopostop head and neck cancer bristol myers squibb + gortec follow-up ongoing disease-free survival 63.1% versus 52.5% in high-risk resected hnscc. asco 2026 post-hoc follow-up dfs 63.1% vs 52.5% at just over 2.5 years; subsequent asco 2026 post-hoc analysis found benefit was not altered by lymph-node dissection extent. the dfs signal persists in follow-up analyses, including across lymph-node dissection extent. no fda approval for the nivopostop regimen was verified; keynote-689 pembrolizumab has separately gained a us perioperative label. adds concurrent and maintenance nivolumab to an already toxic postoperative chemoradiotherapy pathway. opdivo keytruda
sections: DFS 63.1% vs 52.5% at just over 2.5 years; subsequent ASCO 2026 post-hoc analysis found benefit was not altered by lymph-node dissection extent.
Additional subgroup follow-up supports consistency but does not substitute for a regulatory decision.
US: No FDA approval for this exact regimen verified. Europe: No verified authorization. India: Nivolumab is marketed, but this postoperative regimen is not verified as approved.
Adds concurrent and maintenance nivolumab to an already toxic postoperative chemoradiotherapy pathway.
Full publication, regulatory strategy and comparative guideline positioning.
Moderate-high: phase III abstract plus post-hoc follow-up.

entries.title: Niraparib + abiraterone + prednisone

entries.title: CAN-2409 + valacyclovir + radiotherapy
entries.subtitle: PrTK03 · Prostate cancer
entries.snapshot: BLA targeted for Q4 2026; not yet submitted
entries.summary: The pivotal trial was positive, but the product remains investigational. The stated Q4 2026 BLA timing is a company target and not a regulatory decision.

entries.title: OPTIMA genomic de-escalation
entries.subtitle: De-escalation · Breast cancer · UK-led international
entries.summary: This is a high-volume overtreatment question. If the signal holds in the abstract and later publication, many patients could avoid chemotherapy toxicity without materially worsening recurrence outcomes.
entries.headline: Conference coverage reported 94.9% vs 93.7% five-year cancer-free survival in the low-score comparison, supporting noninferiority of omitting chemotherapy.
entries.searchText: optima genomic de-escalation optima de-escalation breast cancer uk-led international ucl + university of glasgow + veracyte prosigna 50-gene assay to omit adjuvant chemotherapy conference coverage reported 94.9% vs 93.7% five-year cancer-free survival in the low-score comparison, supporting noninferiority of omitting chemotherapy. whether multigene testing should be used more broadly to justify skipping chemotherapy in lower-risk disease. this is a high-volume overtreatment question. if the signal holds in the abstract and later publication, many patients could avoid chemotherapy toxicity without materially worsening recurrence outcomes.
sections: Prosigna 50-gene assay to omit adjuvant chemotherapy
4,429 adults aged 40+ with hormone-sensitive early breast cancer after surgery
Usual chemotherapy plus endocrine therapy vs assay-guided chemotherapy omission in low-score patients
Conference coverage reported 94.9% vs 93.7% five-year cancer-free survival in the low-score comparison, supporting noninferiority of omitting chemotherapy.

This is a high-volume overtreatment question. If the signal holds in the abstract and later publication, many patients could avoid chemotherapy toxicity without materially worsening recurrence outcomes.

Randomized international study with thousands of participants and direct patient-level treatment de-escalation relevance.
Potentially important where chemotherapy burden, fertility concerns and capacity constraints are high, but assay access and reimbursement in India were not verified.

entries.title: Galleri-NHS negative screening signal
entries.subtitle: Screening · Multiple cancers · United Kingdom
entries.summary: This is a major cautionary signal for population blood-based multi-cancer screening. A negative primary endpoint should slow premature adoption and reshape how MCED claims are presented to payers and the public.
entries.headline: Conference coverage reported no statistically significant reduction in the combined stage III-IV primary endpoint, although stage IV diagnoses alone reportedly fell by 14%.
entries.searchText: galleri-nhs negative screening signal galleri-nhs screening multiple cancers united kingdom nhs england + grail galleri mced blood test added to routine screening conference coverage reported no statistically significant reduction in the combined stage iii-iv primary endpoint, although stage iv diagnoses alone reportedly fell by 14%. whether mced testing has enough evidence for population-scale implementation before mortality data mature. this is a major cautionary signal for population blood-based multi-cancer screening. a negative primary endpoint should slow premature adoption and reshape how mced claims are presented to payers and the public.
sections: Galleri MCED blood test added to routine screening
142,942 asymptomatic adults aged 50-77 in a randomized NHS study
Standard screening alone vs standard screening plus annual MCED testing
Conference coverage reported no statistically significant reduction in the combined stage III-IV primary endpoint, although stage IV diagnoses alone reportedly fell by 14%.

This is a major cautionary signal for population blood-based multi-cancer screening. A negative primary endpoint should slow premature adoption and reshape how MCED claims are presented to payers and the public.
Asymptomatic adults considering direct-pay testing, screening policymakers, and systems evaluating MCED procurement.
Whether MCED testing has enough evidence for population-scale implementation before mortality data mature.
Very large randomized implementation study with direct policy relevance.
Strong argument against routine rollout or aggressive marketing of MCED testing without proven patient-outcome benefit in local policy contexts.
No official India pricing or government procurement pathway was verified.

entries.title: Durvalumab bladder-preservation signal
entries.subtitle: Surgery / RT · Bladder cancer · United Kingdom
entries.organization: Institute of Cancer Research + Royal Marsden + University of Birmingham + AstraZeneca
entries.summary: For some patients, avoiding radical cystectomy changes daily function and quality of life as much as it changes tumor control. This is a distinctly patient-centered oncology development, not just another drug signal.
entries.headline: Conference coverage reported 46 of 54 patients (85%) without recurrence at one year, versus about 60% in prior chemoradiotherapy studies without immunotherapy.
entries.searchText: durvalumab bladder-preservation signal durvalumab crt surgery / rt bladder cancer united kingdom institute of cancer research + royal marsden + university of birmingham + astrazeneca durvalumab added to chemoradiotherapy to avoid cystectomy conference coverage reported 46 of 54 patients (85%) without recurrence at one year, versus about 60% in prior chemoradiotherapy studies without immunotherapy. whether this approach should stay investigational, expand in trial networks, or influence future bladder-preservation standards. for some patients, avoiding radical cystectomy changes daily function and quality of life as much as it changes tumor control. this is a distinctly patient-centered oncology development, not just another drug signal. imfinzi
sections: Durvalumab added to chemoradiotherapy to avoid cystectomy
54 patients with aggressive or muscle-invasive bladder cancer in a phase II study
Single-arm study against historical chemoradiotherapy experience
Conference coverage reported 46 of 54 patients (85%) without recurrence at one year, versus about 60% in prior chemoradiotherapy studies without immunotherapy.

For some patients, avoiding radical cystectomy changes daily function and quality of life as much as it changes tumor control. This is a distinctly patient-centered oncology development, not just another drug signal.
Patients eligible for bladder-preserving treatment, multidisciplinary uro-oncology teams, and radiotherapy programs.
Whether this approach should stay investigational, expand in trial networks, or influence future bladder-preservation standards.
Meaningful early efficacy in a high-burden setting with direct quality-of-life implications.
Conceptually important where radical surgery has major quality-of-life consequences, but durvalumab cost and exact study-use access remain major barriers.
Durvalumab is marketed in India, but no official price update or bladder-preservation indication was verified in this pass.
Conference-only phase II data with no randomized control captured in this pass. The result is hypothesis-strengthening, not yet label- or guideline-defining.

entries.title: Global oncology workforce warning
entries.subtitle: Workforce · All cancers · Global
entries.organization: Global oncology authorship led from MSK and Queen's University Belfast; Lancet-linked report
entries.summary: Therapeutic progress is not enough if systems cannot diagnose, staff infusion units, read scans, deliver radiotherapy or provide palliative care. This directly affects real-world access and survival.
entries.headline: Conference coverage reported a 21% rise in cancer incidence by 2050, more than 35 million annual cases, and a projected 100 million cancer-care worker shortfall, including about 65 million nurses and 16 million diagnostic staff.
entries.searchText: global oncology workforce warning workforce 2050 workforce all cancers global global oncology authorship led from msk and queen's university belfast; lancet-linked report projected cancer-care workforce shortfall conference coverage reported a 21% rise in cancer incidence by 2050, more than 35 million annual cases, and a projected 100 million cancer-care worker shortfall, including about 65 million nurses and 16 million diagnostic staff. whether countries invest earlier in workforce planning, task-shifting, digital tools and diagnostic capacity instead of treating access failures as downstream surprises. therapeutic progress is not enough if systems cannot diagnose, staff infusion units, read scans, deliver radiotherapy or provide palliative care. this directly affects real-world access and survival.
sections: Global oncology systems, especially countries with diagnostic and nursing shortages
2025 baseline capacity vs 2050 projected incidence and staffing needs

Conference coverage reported a 21% rise in cancer incidence by 2050, more than 35 million annual cases, and a projected 100 million cancer-care worker shortfall, including about 65 million nurses and 16 million diagnostic staff.
Therapeutic progress is not enough if systems cannot diagnose, staff infusion units, read scans, deliver radiotherapy or provide palliative care. This directly affects real-world access and survival.
Health ministries, hospital networks, pathology/radiology training pipelines, and patients in both LMIC and high-income settings.
Whether countries invest earlier in workforce planning, task-shifting, digital tools and diagnostic capacity instead of treating access failures as downstream surprises.
Large-scale system modeling and commission-style analysis with direct care-delivery relevance.
Highly relevant for oncology manpower, pathology, radiology and geographic access planning.

This is a projection, not an intervention trial. The full Lancet text was not retrieved in this pass, so country-level assumptions and model sensitivity need direct review before more granular claims are made.
Retrieve the full report and map implications to India-specific workforce and infrastructure gaps.
